Title: Create an outbound referral to a specialty with all required fields
Preconditions: Logged in as a clinician with referral privilege on an active patient with an open visit
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient dashboard | 2. Start a new referral order | 3. Select referring provider | 4. Select target specialty | 5. Select target facility | 6. Enter reason for referral | 7. Set urgency Routine | 8. Save the referral
Test_Data: Patient=Jane Synthetic (ID=10001V); Specialty=Cardiology; Facility=City Heart Center; Reason=Evaluate murmur; Urgency=Routine
Expected_Result: Referral is created with a unique referral ID, status Requested, captured provider, specialty, facility, reason and urgency, and appears on the patient's referral list

Title: Referral creation blocked when target specialty is missing
Preconditions: Logged in clinician on the new referral screen for an active patient
Test_Steps: 1. Start a new referral | 2. Leave target specialty empty | 3. Enter reason and facility | 4. Attempt to Save
Test_Data: Patient=Jane Synthetic (ID=10001V); Specialty=(blank); Facility=City Heart Center; Reason=Evaluate murmur
Expected_Result: Save is rejected with a validation message that specialty is required; no referral record is persisted

Title: Referral creation blocked when reason for referral is empty
Preconditions: Logged in clinician on the new referral screen for an active patient
Test_Steps: 1. Start a new referral | 2. Select specialty and facility | 3. Leave reason blank | 4. Attempt to Save
Expected_Result: Save is rejected with a validation message that a clinical reason is required; no referral is created

Title: Reason for referral free text accepts maximum allowed length at boundary
Preconditions: Logged in clinician on the new referral screen for an active patient
Test_Steps: 1. Start a new referral | 2. Select specialty and facility | 3. Paste reason text exactly at the maximum allowed character count | 4. Save | 5. Reopen the referral
Expected_Result: Referral saves successfully and the full reason text is stored and rendered without truncation

Title: Reason for referral exceeding maximum length is rejected or truncated with warning
Preconditions: Logged in clinician on the new referral screen for an active patient
Test_Steps: 1. Start a new referral | 2. Enter reason text one character over the maximum | 3. Attempt to Save
Expected_Result: System either blocks save with a length validation message or enforces the limit at the field; no silent data loss occurs

Title: Referral cannot be created for a deceased patient
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; patient record flagged deceased
Test_Steps: 1. Open the deceased patient record | 2. Attempt to start a new referral | 3. Observe system response
Expected_Result: System blocks or warns against creating a referral for a deceased patient; if blocked no record is created and a clear message is shown

Title: Select specialty filters the available target facilities
Preconditions: Logged in clinician on new referral screen; facility directory configured with specialty mappings
Test_Steps: 1. Start a new referral | 2. Select specialty Oncology | 3. Open the target facility list | 4. Observe available facilities
Expected_Result: Only facilities offering the selected specialty are listed; facilities without that specialty are excluded

Title: Out-of-network facility shows network warning before save
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; insurance plan with defined network; facility directory tagged in/out of network
Test_Steps: 1. Start a referral for an insured patient | 2. Select an out-of-network facility | 3. Attempt to Save
Test_Data: Patient=Insured Synthetic (ID=10011V); Plan=PPO Synthetic; Facility=Out-of-network Clinic
Expected_Result: An out-of-network warning is displayed; clinician must acknowledge before the referral is saved; acknowledgement is recorded

Title: Specialty and facility selection pairwise combinations
Preconditions: Logged in clinician on new referral screen with multiple specialties and facilities configured
Test_Steps: 1. For each pair create a referral | 2. Select specialty | 3. Select facility | 4. Save and verify mapping
Test_Data: Pairs: Cardiology/City Heart; Oncology/Cancer Inst; Neurology/Neuro Center; Cardiology/Cancer Inst (invalid)
Expected_Result: Valid specialty-facility pairs save; the invalid pair (facility lacking specialty) is rejected with a clear message

Title: Set urgency to STAT triggers high-priority routing
Preconditions: Logged in clinician on new referral; urgency options configured Routine, Urgent, STAT
Test_Steps: 1. Start a referral | 2. Set urgency STAT | 3. Enter clinical reason | 4. Save | 5. Check referral queue priority
Test_Data: Patient=Jane Synthetic (ID=10001V); Urgency=STAT; Reason=Acute chest pain
Expected_Result: Referral is saved with urgency STAT and surfaces at the top of the receiving queue with a high-priority indicator

Title: Urgency decision table maps to required response SLA
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; SLA configuration defined per urgency
Test_Steps: 1. Create referrals with each urgency value | 2. Observe assigned response-by date | 3. Verify against SLA table
Expected_Result: Each urgency value yields the correct response-by deadline per the SLA decision table; deadline is stored on the referral

Title: Default urgency applied when none selected
Preconditions: Logged in clinician on new referral screen
Test_Steps: 1. Start a referral | 2. Do not change the urgency field | 3. Save | 4. Inspect stored urgency
Expected_Result: Referral saves with the configured default urgency (Routine) and the default is clearly indicated to the user

Title: Attach a supporting clinical document to a referral
Preconditions: Logged in clinician with an existing referral in Requested status; document upload enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Open the referral | 2. Click Attach document | 3. Select a PDF clinical summary | 4. Upload | 5. Confirm attachment listed
Expected_Result: Document uploads successfully, is listed against the referral with filename, size, uploader and timestamp, and is viewable

Title: Reject document upload exceeding maximum file size
Preconditions: Logged in clinician on a referral; max upload size configured
Test_Steps: 1. Open the referral | 2. Attach a file larger than the configured maximum | 3. Attempt upload
Expected_Result: Upload is rejected with a clear file-size error; no partial file is stored against the referral

Title: Reject disallowed file type on referral attachment
Preconditions: Logged in clinician on a referral; allowed types restricted to PDF, JPG, PNG
Test_Steps: 1. Open the referral | 2. Attempt to attach an executable file | 3. Observe response
Expected_Result: Upload is blocked with an unsupported file type message; executable content is never stored or served

Title: Uploaded attachment is access-controlled by patient privacy rules
Preconditions: Two clinicians; one without access to the patient's restricted record
Test_Steps: 1. Clinician A attaches a document to a referral | 2. Clinician B without patient access attempts to open the attachment via direct URL
Expected_Result: Clinician B is denied access to the attachment; access attempt is logged in the audit trail

Title: Receiving provider accepts a referral
Preconditions: Logged in as receiving provider; referral in Requested status assigned to their facility
Test_Steps: 1. Open the receiving referral queue | 2. Open referral REF-1001 | 3. Click Accept | 4. Add scheduling note | 5. Confirm
Expected_Result: Referral transitions from Requested to Accepted; acceptance timestamp and accepting provider are recorded; referring provider is notified

Title: Receiving provider rejects a referral with a reason
Preconditions: Logged in as receiving provider; referral in Requested status
Test_Steps: 1. Open referral REF-1002 | 2. Click Reject | 3. Select rejection reason | 4. Add free-text note | 5. Confirm
Expected_Result: Referral transitions to Rejected; reason and note are stored; referring provider is notified to take corrective action

Title: Rejection without a reason is blocked
Preconditions: Logged in as receiving provider; referral in Requested status; reason mandatory
Test_Steps: 1. Open referral REF-1002 | 2. Click Reject | 3. Leave reason blank | 4. Confirm
Expected_Result: Rejection is blocked with a validation message that a rejection reason is required; status remains Requested

Title: Complete an accepted referral after the consult
Preconditions: Logged in as receiving provider; referral in Accepted status with consult performed
Test_Steps: 1. Open referral REF-1001 | 2. Enter consult outcome summary | 3. Click Complete | 4. Confirm
Test_Data: Referral=REF-1001; Outcome=Cardiology consult done, plan attached
Expected_Result: Referral transitions from Accepted to Completed; completion timestamp and outcome are recorded; referring provider is notified

Title: Invalid transition from Requested directly to Completed is blocked
Preconditions: Referral in Requested status; user with privilege to update status
Test_Steps: 1. Open referral REF-1003 in Requested | 2. Attempt to set status Completed without Accept | 3. Observe
Expected_Result: Direct transition is blocked; the system enforces the valid lifecycle Requested then Accepted then Completed; status unchanged

Title: Cancel a referral before acceptance
Preconditions: Referring clinician; referral in Requested status
Test_Steps: 1. Open referral REF-1004 | 2. Click Cancel | 3. Select cancellation reason | 4. Confirm
Expected_Result: Referral transitions from Requested to Cancelled; reason and timestamp recorded; referral removed from active receiving queue

Title: Cannot modify a completed referral
Preconditions: Referral in Completed status
Test_Steps: 1. Open referral REF-1001 in Completed | 2. Attempt to edit specialty or reason | 3. Observe
Expected_Result: Editing is blocked; completed referrals are read-only; an addendum mechanism may be offered but core fields are immutable

Title: Reactivate a rejected referral after correction
Preconditions: Referral in Rejected status; referring clinician with corrected information
Test_Steps: 1. Open rejected referral REF-1002 | 2. Add missing clinical information | 3. Resubmit | 4. Verify status
Expected_Result: Referral transitions from Rejected back to Requested with a new submission timestamp; prior rejection is retained in history

Title: Full referral lifecycle end to end
Preconditions: Referring and receiving providers configured; patient with active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Create referral | 2. Receiving provider accepts | 3. Consult performed | 4. Receiving provider completes with outcome | 5. Feedback returned to referrer | 6. Verify history
Expected_Result: All transitions Requested, Accepted, Completed with feedback occur; each state change is timestamped, attributed and visible in the referral history timeline

Title: Initiate inter-facility transfer of care for an admitted patient
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; patient currently admitted; receiving facility configured
Test_Steps: 1. Open the admitted patient | 2. Start transfer of care | 3. Select receiving facility and accepting service | 4. Attach transfer summary | 5. Submit
Expected_Result: Transfer of care request is created with sending and receiving facilities, accepting service, transfer summary and status Pending Acceptance

Title: Transfer summary must include active medications and allergies
Preconditions: Patient with documented active medications and allergies; transfer in progress
Test_Steps: 1. Initiate transfer of care | 2. Generate transfer summary | 3. Inspect summary content
Expected_Result: Transfer summary includes current medications, allergies, active problems and recent results; missing safety data triggers a completeness warning

Title: Receiving facility accepts the transfer and assumes care
Preconditions: Transfer in Pending Acceptance status; receiving facility user
Test_Steps: 1. Open incoming transfer queue at receiving facility | 2. Review transfer summary | 3. Accept transfer | 4. Assign bed and accepting provider
Expected_Result: Transfer transitions to Accepted; accepting provider and bed recorded; sending facility notified; care responsibility flag updated

Title: Transfer rejected due to no bed availability
Preconditions: Transfer in Pending Acceptance; receiving facility at capacity
Test_Steps: 1. Open incoming transfer TRN-2002 | 2. Reject with reason No capacity | 3. Confirm
Expected_Result: Transfer transitions to Rejected with capacity reason; sending facility notified to seek alternative; patient care responsibility remains with sender

Title: Receiving provider returns consult feedback to the referrer
Preconditions: Referral in Completed status; consult notes available
Test_Steps: 1. Open completed referral REF-1001 | 2. Compose feedback report | 3. Attach findings and recommendations | 4. Send to referring provider
Expected_Result: Feedback report is delivered to the referring provider, linked to the referral, and visible on the referring patient record

Title: Referring provider receives notification when feedback arrives
Preconditions: Referring provider with notifications enabled; feedback sent
Test_Steps: 1. Receiving provider sends feedback | 2. Referring provider checks notification inbox
Expected_Result: Referring provider receives a notification linking to the feedback; unread state clears when opened

Title: Overdue referral with no feedback is escalated
Preconditions: Referral past its response-by SLA with no feedback
Test_Steps: 1. Create referral with short SLA | 2. Allow SLA deadline to pass | 3. Run escalation job | 4. Check escalation queue
Expected_Result: Overdue referral is flagged and escalated per the SLA decision table; escalation notification is sent to the responsible party

Title: Feedback loop closes referral and updates patient timeline
Preconditions: Referral Completed with feedback returned and acknowledged
Test_Steps: 1. Referrer opens feedback | 2. Acknowledge feedback | 3. Verify referral state and timeline
Expected_Result: Referral marked Closed after acknowledgement; patient timeline shows the closed loop with consult outcome

Title: Create FHIR ServiceRequest for a referral via API
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint available; OAuth token with write scope; patient resource exists
Test_Steps: 1. POST a ServiceRequest with intent order and category referral | 2. Reference subject, requester, performerType | 3. Read back the created resource
Expected_Result: ServiceRequest is created with HTTP 201, a server-assigned id, status active, and all referenced elements resolve correctly

Title: Reject ServiceRequest with invalid intent code
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint; valid token
Test_Steps: 1. POST a ServiceRequest with an invalid intent value | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: Server returns HTTP 400 or 422 with an OperationOutcome identifying the invalid intent against the required value set

Title: Map referral urgency to ServiceRequest.priority
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint; referrals created with each urgency
Test_Steps: 1. Create referrals Routine, Urgent, STAT | 2. Retrieve their ServiceRequest resources | 3. Compare priority element
Expected_Result: ServiceRequest.priority maps correctly to routine, urgent and stat respectively per the FHIR value set

Title: Search ServiceRequest by patient and category referral
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint; multiple ServiceRequests for the patient
Test_Steps: 1. GET /fhir/ServiceRequest?subject=Patient/10001V&category=referral | 2. Inspect bundle
Expected_Result: A searchset Bundle returns only referral-category ServiceRequests for the patient; total and entries are consistent

Title: ServiceRequest status transitions reflect referral lifecycle
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint; referral progressing through lifecycle
Test_Steps: 1. Create referral and read ServiceRequest.status | 2. Accept and re-read | 3. Complete and re-read | 4. Cancel another and re-read
Test_Data: Lifecycle states mapped to active, completed, revoked
Expected_Result: ServiceRequest.status reflects active on request, completed on completion and revoked on cancellation per FHIR semantics

Title: Unauthorized token cannot create ServiceRequest
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint; token lacking write scope
Test_Steps: 1. POST a ServiceRequest with a read-only token | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: Server returns HTTP 401 or 403; no resource is created; the denied attempt is auditable

Title: Outbound REF I12 referral message generated for an external referral
Preconditions: HL7 interface engine connected; referral configured to send REF message
Test_Steps: 1. Create an external referral | 2. Trigger HL7 export | 3. Capture the outbound message | 4. Parse segments
Expected_Result: A well-formed REF^I12 message is generated with correct PID, provider (PRD) and referral information (RF1) segments and valid MSH control fields

Title: Inbound referral acknowledgement ACK is processed
Preconditions: HL7 interface active; outbound REF sent awaiting ACK
Test_Steps: 1. Send outbound REF^I12 | 2. Receive ACK from partner | 3. Verify referral status update
Expected_Result: On receipt of an Application Accept ACK the referral records successful transmission; an Application Error ACK flags the referral for retry

Title: Malformed inbound referral message is rejected and logged
Preconditions: HL7 inbound channel configured
Test_Steps: 1. Send a REF message with a missing required PID segment | 2. Observe interface handling
Expected_Result: Message is rejected with a negative acknowledgement (AE/AR); error is logged; no partial referral is created

Title: Clinician without referral privilege cannot create a referral
Preconditions: User assigned a role lacking referral creation privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as the restricted user | 2. Open a patient | 3. Attempt to start a referral
Expected_Result: The referral action is hidden or disabled; direct attempts are denied with an authorization error and logged

Title: Receiving-queue access restricted to assigned facility
Preconditions: Two facilities; user assigned only to Facility A
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as Facility A user | 2. Attempt to open Facility B receiving queue
Expected_Result: User cannot view or action referrals assigned to Facility B; cross-facility access is denied and audited

Title: All referral state changes are recorded in the audit trail
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; referral progressing through lifecycle
Test_Steps: 1. Create, accept and complete a referral | 2. Query the audit/log table for the referral id
Expected_Result: Each create and state change has an audit entry with actor, timestamp, old and new status, and is immutable

Title: Referral persists referential integrity to patient, provider and facility
Preconditions: Database access; a saved referral
Test_Steps: 1. Query the referral record | 2. Verify foreign keys to patient, requester and facility resolve | 3. Attempt to delete a referenced patient
Expected_Result: Foreign keys resolve to valid records; deleting a patient referenced by a referral is prevented or cascades per policy without orphaning the referral

Title: Concurrent accept by two providers does not double-process
Preconditions: Referral in Requested; two receiving users open it simultaneously
Test_Steps: 1. Both users open REF-1006 | 2. Both click Accept near-simultaneously | 3. Observe outcome
Expected_Result: Optimistic locking or equivalent prevents double acceptance; one accept succeeds, the other receives a stale-state error; final status is consistent

Title: New referral form is fully keyboard navigable
Preconditions: Referral module open; keyboard only
Test_Steps: 1. Tab through every field on the new referral form | 2. Operate specialty, facility, urgency selectors via keyboard | 3. Submit via keyboard
Expected_Result: All controls are reachable and operable by keyboard in a logical order with visible focus; the form submits without a mouse

Title: Referral status and urgency are conveyed without color alone
Preconditions: Referral list with mixed statuses and urgencies; screen reader available
Test_Steps: 1. Open the referral list | 2. Inspect status and urgency indicators | 3. Verify with a screen reader
Test_Data: STAT, Urgent, Routine; statuses Requested, Accepted, Rejected
Expected_Result: Status and urgency are conveyed by text or icon plus accessible name, not color alone; screen reader announces them correctly

Title: Exploratory session on referral edge cases and interruptions
Preconditions: Test environment with referral module; charter defined
Test_Steps: 1. Time-box a session exploring duplicate referrals, browser back during save, network drop mid-attach, rapid status toggling | 2. Record anomalies
Test_Data: Charter: referral creation and lifecycle robustness
Expected_Result: No data corruption, orphaned attachments, duplicate referrals or stuck states are found; anomalies are logged with reproduction steps

Title: Duplicate referral to same specialty and facility within window is warned
Preconditions: Patient with an existing open referral to a specialty; duplicate-check configured
Test_Steps: 1. Create a second referral to the same specialty and facility while the first is open | 2. Observe
Test_Data: Patient=Jane Synthetic (ID=10001V); Specialty=Cardiology; Facility=City Heart Center
Expected_Result: A possible-duplicate warning is shown requiring confirmation; if confirmed the referral is created and linked to the prior one

Title: STAT referral created outside business hours still routes for action
Preconditions: On-call routing configured; current time outside business hours
Test_Steps: 1. Create a STAT referral after hours | 2. Verify routing target
Expected_Result: STAT referral routes to the on-call recipient regardless of business hours; routine referrals queue for next business day

Title: Patient consent captured before sharing record on external transfer
Preconditions: External transfer to a non-affiliated facility; consent module enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Initiate external transfer | 2. System prompts for consent to share PHI | 3. Capture or decline consent
Expected_Result: Transfer proceeds only after consent to share PHI is recorded; if declined, only minimal necessary data is shared or transfer is blocked per policy

Title: ServiceRequest supportingInfo references attached documents
Preconditions: Referral with an attached document; FHIR endpoint
Test_Steps: 1. Create referral with attachment | 2. Retrieve the ServiceRequest | 3. Inspect supportingInfo references
Expected_Result: ServiceRequest.supportingInfo references resolvable DocumentReference resources for the attachments

Title: Feedback report attaches structured outcome coded value
Preconditions: Receiving provider completing a referral; outcome coded list configured
Test_Steps: 1. Complete referral and select outcome from coded list | 2. Save feedback | 3. Verify stored code
Test_Data: Outcomes: Resolved, Ongoing management, Re-referred, No-show
Expected_Result: The selected outcome is stored as a coded value mapping correctly to the configured concept; reporting can aggregate by outcome
